name: Peña de Francia from Maíllo — circular by Monsago
name_original: Peña de Francia desde Maíllo — circular por Monsago
description: The Peña de Francia from Maíllo — circular by Monsago is a difficult 30.0-mile touring cycling route with 3602 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Maíllo, located in the province of Salamanca, Spain, offers routes through varied terrain. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, including the Sierras de Tamames y Quilamas and the Sierra de Francia Natural Park, which contribute to significant elevation gains. Cyclists can expect demanding climbs and descents, with some routes featuring well-paved surfaces suitable for road cycling. The area provides access to picturesque landscapes, including forests and valleys. Best touring cycling routes around El Maíllo The most popular touring cycling route is Peña de Francia from Maíllo — circular by Monsago, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant elevation gain of over 3,500 feet. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Alberca Main Square – Portillo Viewpoint loop from El Maíllo, a difficult 44.4 miles (71.4 km) path. This route offers extensive views and challenging climbs through the region's varied topography. Local touring cyclists also love the Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia — circular desde La Alberca, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Francia Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 0 minutes. Touring cycling around El Maíllo is defined by mountainous landscapes, natural parks, and varied topography. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant climbs. The routes in El Maíllo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 280 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Maíllo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around El Maíllo?
A: Touring cycling around El Maíllo features varied and often challenging terrain. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, including the Sierras de Tamames y Quilamas and the Sierra de Francia Natural Park. You can expect significant elevation gains, demanding climbs, and descents. While some routes have well-paved surfaces suitable for road cycling, the area provides access to picturesque landscapes with forests and valleys.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in El Maíllo for beginners?
A: While many routes around El Maíllo feature significant climbs and are rated as difficult, there is at least one route categorized as easy. Overall, the network offers options for different ability levels, but beginners should carefully check the elevation profiles before setting out.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: El Maíllo is known for its challenging routes. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19807122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alberca Main Square – Portillo Viewpoint loop from El Maíllo&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 71.4 km (44.4 miles) path offering extensive views and significant climbs. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319394266/pena-de-francia-from-maillo-circular-by-monsago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Francia from Maíllo — circular by Monsago&lt;/a&gt;, a 48.3 km (30.0 miles) trail with over 1,090 meters (3,500 feet) of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region around El Maíllo offers several notable landmarks and natural features. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1590368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of the Desert of San José de Las Batuecas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1043021535/parque-natural-de-las-batuecas-y-sierra-de-francia-circular-desde-la-alberca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia — circular desde La Alberca&lt;/a&gt; is a 41.2 km (25.6 miles) loop through the Sierra de Francia Natural Park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40637835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wolves' Pass – Peña de Francia loop from Cereceda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 38.2 km (23.7 miles) circular route.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in El Maíllo?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Maíllo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and the scenic beauty of the mountainous landscapes and natural parks.

Q: Is El Maíllo a good destination for touring cyclists looking for a significant challenge?
A: Absolutely. El Maíllo is an excellent destination for touring cyclists seeking a challenge. The region's mountainous terrain, including the Sierras de Tamames y Quilamas and the Sierra de Francia Natural Park, ensures routes with substantial elevation gains and demanding sections. Many of the 17 available routes are rated as difficult, providing a rigorous and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Sierra de Francia Natural Park?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1043021535/parque-natural-de-las-batuecas-y-sierra-de-francia-circular-desde-la-alberca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia — circular desde La Alberca&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route that leads directly through the Sierra de Francia Natural Park, offering cyclists a chance to experience its unique landscapes and natural beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in El Maíllo?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for demanding climbs, spring and autumn are generally the most favorable seasons for touring cycling in El Maíllo. During these times, temperatures are typically milder than the peak summer heat, making long rides more comfortable. Always check local weather forecasts before your trip.

Q: Are there any routes that offer extensive views?
A: Yes, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19807122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alberca Main Square – Portillo Viewpoint loop from El Maíllo&lt;/a&gt; are specifically noted for offering extensive views. The region's mountainous topography naturally lends itself to numerous scenic vistas, especially from higher elevations and viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1590368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Maíllo?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around El Maíllo, offering a variety of options for different skill levels, though the majority are categorized as difficult due to the region's mountainous nature.
tips:
Not only does it give its name to the wonderful Natural Park, but its peak at 1,727 meters above sea level offers the best panoramic views in all directions. Right there, at the top, is the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Peña de Francia of the Dominican Order that is worth visiting. And of course, you cannot return without having taken the obligatory photos at the viewpoint and the Balcony of Santiago, two panoramic points par excellence.
You can get here by several very picturesque paths and trails of varying difficulty.
Great viewpoint of the Agadon Valley, a mandatory stop on the way up to Peña de Francia or the descent, to take more photos and read interesting facts on the information panel.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered difficult, requiring a very good level of physical fitness. It features a significant elevation gain of around 1,100 meters, with the climb to Peña de Francia itself being particularly demanding.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Peña de Francia circular touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists generally complete this 48.3-kilometer route in about 3 hours and 31 minutes, though this can vary based on fitness levels and stops.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Maíllo. While specific parking details for Maíllo aren't provided, it's a common starting point for this circular route. Look for available parking within the village.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Peña de Francia circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is described as 'mostly paved,' but be aware that some segments may be unpaved or challenging for certain bike types, potentially requiring you to push your bike in places. The climb to Peña de Francia is steep and demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking panoramic views, especially from the summit of Peña de Francia. The route passes through diverse landscapes, including river valleys, forests of alders, heather, and rockrose, and dense oak and pine forests, offering varied mountain colors and opportunities to spot black vultures.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights along the Peña de Francia touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the challenging ascent to Peña de Francia, offering expansive views, and passing through Los Lobos Pass (1,499 m). The route also takes you through the picturesque Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park and the unique village of Monsagro, known for its fossil-decorated houses.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring challenging conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific information regarding dog-friendliness for touring cycling on this particular trail is not available. It's always best to check local regulations for the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park regarding pets on trails.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any fees to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that permits or entrance fees are required for cycling this route. It passes through the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park, but typically, general access for cycling does not require a permit.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get supplies near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through villages like Maíllo and Monsagro. Monsagro is a notable point of interest, and you can expect to find basic amenities, cafes, or restaurants in these villages, especially in Maíllo, which is a larger starting point.

faqs.question: What should I know about the challenging climb to Peña de Francia?
faqs.answer: The climb to Peña de Francia from Monsagro is particularly demanding. It covers 16.08 kilometers with an average gradient of 4.86% and a total ascent of 781 meters, classifying it as a Category 1 climb. Be prepared for a strenuous effort and ensure your bike is suitable for steep ascents.
